perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. Member shall not, during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter, communicate, divulge or use, for any purpose other than the operation of the Franchised Business, any confidential information, knowledge, trade secrets or know-how which may be communicated to Member or which Member may learn by virtue of Member's relationship with Franchisee. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System ("Confidential Information") is deemed confidential, except for information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member; or which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. Member shall not, during the initial term of the Franchise Agreement, communicate, divulge or use confidential information. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System (“Confidential Information”) is deemed confidential, except for information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member; or which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits the use of confidential information 'during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter'. The changed text restricts this prohibition to 'during the initial term of the Franchise Agreement', creating an ambiguity. This contradicts the general intent of protecting confidential information even after the initial term as implicitly suggested in Section 2's non-compete clause, which extends beyond the term of the agreement in some cases. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Covenants Not to Compete. (a) Member specifically acknowledges that, pursuant to the Franchise Agreement, and by virtue of its position with Franchisee, Member will receive valuable specialized training and Confidential Information, including, without limitation, information regarding the operational, sales, promotional, and marketing methods and techniques of Franchisor and the System. (b) Member covenants and agrees that during the term of the Franchise Agreement, except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, persons, partnership, corporation, or entity: (i) Own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, rent or lease to, or have any other interest in business that (directly or indirectly) operates, or grants franchises or licenses to operate, a restaurant featuring pizza and related food specialties or that offers products or services substantially similar to those then offered by Pizza Fusions Restaurants ("Competitive Business"); C - 1 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 (ii) Divert or attempt to divert any business or customer, or potential business or customer, to any Competitive Business; or (iii) Induce any person to leave his or her employment with Franchisee or Franchisor. (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System. (d) As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement (regardless of the cause for termination); (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity).
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1 prohibited the use of confidential information both 'during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter'. The changed text limits this prohibition to 'during the initial term of the Franchise Agreement'. Section 2, however, includes a non-compete clause that extends beyond the term of the agreement ('Post-Term Period' defined as two years from termination, expiration, etc.). This creates a clear contradiction: Section 1 now allows the Member to use confidential information after the initial term, while Section 2 restricts the Member's ability to compete, which relies on the confidentiality of information received during the franchise agreement, even after the initial term. Thus, the modified Section 1 directly conflicts with Section 2 regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, making enforcement uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System' the scope of the non-compete agreement post-term becomes ambiguous. It is no longer clear if the non-compete applies only within a specific radius or applies without geographical limitation. This directly contradicts the defined geographical limitations elsewhere in the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction especially considering the definition of 'Post-Term Period' still relies on events related to the franchise location within those geographic constraints.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement (regardless of the cause for termination); (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the geographic limitations on the non-compete clause, while the 'Post-Term Period' definition in the contradicted text is still tied to events and locations within the original geographic scope. This creates a contradiction because the non-compete's duration is defined based on the franchise location but the scope of the non-compete is now undefined geographically, potentially covering a much broader area than originally intended. The removal creates an ambiguity that directly conflicts with the defined term, making it unenforceable due to the lack of clarity on geographic scope.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Delay. No delay or failure by the Franchisor or Franchisee to exercise any right under this Agreement, and no partial or single exercise of that right, shall constitute a waiver of that or any other right provided herein, and no waiver of any violation of any terms and provisions of this Agreement shall be construed as a waiver of any succeeding violation of the same or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Delay. No delay or failure by the Franchisor or Franchisee to exercise any right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of that right. However, if any violation of any terms and provisions of this Agreement is waived, it shall be construed as a waiver of any succeeding violation of the same or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that no waiver of any violation shall be construed as a waiver of any succeeding violation. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that any waiver of any violation *shall* be construed as a waiver of any succeeding violation. This in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the agreement, as repeated minor breaches may be argued as having implicitly waived future enforcement of the clause. This particularly affects clauses regarding payment and operation standards that are referenced elswhere in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Delay. No delay or failure by the Franchisor or Franchisee to exercise any right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of that right. However, if any violation of any terms and provisions of this Agreement is waived, it shall be construed as a waiver of any succeeding violation of the same or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5 directly contradicts the original intent of section 5 regarding waiver of rights. The original text explicitly states that waiving a violation does *not* imply waiving subsequent violations. The changed text reverses this, stating that waiving a violation *does* imply waiving subsequent violations. Since the contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text in this case, the contradiction is present within the same section due to the change made. This creates an in-text contradiction, as defined by the prompt, making the contract unenforceable.
